critical_invariants: DMA alignment: source and destination addresses must meet GPU alignment requirements (typically 256 bytes)
Fence synchronization: CPU must not access DMA target memory until fence signals completion
DMA transfer size must not exceed hardware maximum per-transfer limit
CLFLUSH/CLWB on source buffer before DMA to ensure cache coherency with GPU
DMA descriptor format must match GPU DMA engine specification exactly
Fence value must monotonically increase - reuse causes race conditions
WBINVD must not be used during active DMA - invalidating cache with in-flight DMA corrupts data
Physical addresses must be used for DMA, not virtual addresses
known_opportunities: DMA size optimization: use largest possible transfer granularity (4KB pages vs byte-by-byte)
Scatter-gather: chain multiple non-contiguous buffers into single DMA operation
Double-buffering: overlap DMA transfer N with CPU preparation of transfer N+1
Fence batching: single fence for multiple sequential DMA operations
MOVNTDQA (non-temporal load) for reading back DMA results to avoid cache pollution
Pinned memory pool: pre-allocate DMA-capable buffers to avoid repeated pinning overhead
Async DMA: fire-and-forget with deferred fence check to maximize CPU/GPU overlap
DMA chunk size tuning: find optimal size that balances setup overhead vs throughput
